benchmark.name: DPM red-team assessment recreation
benchmark.one_liner: Recreate a regulated-domain red-team assessment with deterministic projection memory, then show whether the agent preserves evidence, cites facts, and recovers from drift under tight memory budgets.
benchmark.hypothesis: A stateless DPM agent should retain audit-critical facts and produce reproducible decisions while reducing prefill cost versus summary-based memory.
benchmark.current_status: Benchmark harness exists; checked-in hardware baselines are still pending.

scenario.description: Synthetic insurance-liability events include invoices, policy limits, corrections, and compliance anchors.
scenario.evidence: dpm_prefill_bench generates an approximately 27k-character event log.

scenario.description: The projection receives a constrained memory budget and must keep legally material facts.
The raw log is reframed into a schema-anchored JSON view with one-based citations.
scenario.evidence: Projection prompt schema includes Facts, Reasoning, and Compliance arrays.

scenario.description: The benchmark should score retained facts, citation integrity, contradiction handling, and reproducibility.
scenario.evidence: Correctness scoring is recommended but not yet checked in with the prefill harness.

scenario.description: The same scenario should be run across CPU/XNNPack and NPU/ML Drift backends.
scenario.evidence: Baseline JSON is pending hardware-owned measurements.

readiness.recommendation: Lead with the red-team recreation: what an assessor tried to break, what evidence mattered, and how DPM responded.
Add callouts, uncertainty labels, and chart annotations that explain why each metric matters.
Capture CPU/XNNPack and NPU/ML Drift runs with model, device, delegate cache, warmup, and iteration metadata.
Add fact-retention, citation-validity, contradiction-resolution, and reproducibility scores next to speed charts.
Publish raw result JSON and summary aggregates with commit SHA, model hash, hardware SKU, and run timestamp.

chart_assessment.today: Charts risk reading as generic performance visuals without the red-team setup.
chart_assessment.improve: Use a top-to-bottom narrative: adversarial log, memory pressure, DPM projection, assessor checks, hardware cost.
chart_assessment.data_needed: Scenario manifest and scored red-team rubric.

chart_assessment.today: Interactive charts are high quality, but the first screen should identify the headline result and evidence quality.
chart_assessment.improve: Add a hero claim, confidence labels, status chips, and short interpretation text beside each chart.
chart_assessment.data_needed: Headline metrics with confidence intervals or pending-state labels.

chart_assessment.today: Prefill throughput alone cannot prove the memory system passes the assessment.
chart_assessment.improve: Pair latency/tokens-per-second with retained-fact accuracy, citation validity, and deterministic replay rate.
chart_assessment.data_needed: Evaluator outputs over repeated seeded runs.

chart_assessment.today: The benchmark runner can repeat iterations, but the page does not yet surface run-to-run variance.
chart_assessment.improve: Show p50/p95, min/max, sample count, and a raw-run hover table for each backend.
chart_assessment.data_needed: Multiple iterations per backend and stable hardware labels.

recommended_metrics.why_it_matters: Shows whether stateless replay is usable under assessor-style long logs.
Normalizes speed across prompt sizes and models.
Validates that compressed projection keeps audit-critical facts.
Prevents unverifiable claims in regulated decisions.
recommended_metrics.source: citation checker over one-based event references

recommended_metrics.why_it_matters: Supports audit replay and legal defensibility.
Measures whether correction events repair state before the next decision.
